rationale: The citing paper uses Ataman, Van Heerde, and Mela (2010) as a key national-brand benchmark when developing expectations about marketing-mix effectiveness, especially the role of product-line length/assortment. It then positions its private-label umbrella rebranding results against that benchmark, explicitly arguing that assortment effectiveness can decrease in this different context. The citation is also used in model setup choices (e.g., treatment of assortment-related baseline support and seasonal controls), indicating functional influence beyond a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing study draws on the cited paper's long-run national-brand marketing-mix findings as a baseline and then qualifies them in a private-label umbrella rebranding setting. In particular, it narrows the generality of the cited paper's positive assortment/product-line implication by showing reduced assortment effectiveness after rebranding.
